cfo_summary: Total available cash sits at $169,564 across all entities. After this week's approved payments of $9,131, projected end-of-week balance is $160,433.

Two entities run thin. Country Aire projects to $6,748 and Winterwood projects to $4,041 after this week's bills. Neither has known incoming deposits before next payroll. Recommend monitoring both before approving any non-essential bills next week.

April BvA shows real concerns. Countryside expenses are pacing 94% over budget with most of the variance in unallocated 'NEED RECEIPT' items totaling $53K. Riverside is pacing 144% over expense budget driven by electric service costs of $7,794. RMF is pacing 138% over expense budget. These need explanations before month-end close.

GRP is carrying the working capital load with $55K projected after $5,330 in maintenance payouts. The watch list shows $52K to Trent and $45K to Tim still unfunded. We cannot fund both distributions and the CA home purchase from current GRP cash.
The bigger forward concern is the $129K in unfunded lien releases tied to the FS2 sale conversions. These are on hold pending sale closings, but the timing creates risk.

for_ownership.label: Projected end-of-week cash
for_ownership.paragraph: We close this week at roughly $160K available across all accounts after $9K in maintenance and contractor bills go out. April expenses are running ahead of budget at four entities, primarily uncategorized items at Countryside and Riverside that need receipt allocation before month close. The CA home closes 5/1 which brings in $35K. Two distributions and the flood plane work remain unfunded. We should discuss timing in Thursday's meeting.

next_month_preview: May payroll lands the second week and runs roughly $26K across entities. GRP and GG should both be in position to cover their share.
CA home sale closes 5/1 which converts the $35K from receivable to cash and frees CA from its current thin position.
If the FS2 flood plane work moves forward in May, that is $38K out of FS or GRP. We should decide funding source before approving the contractor.
Need receipt allocation cleanup is the first May priority. $53K of Countryside expense and $7K of GRP expense sit in 'NEED RECEIPT' lines and must be reclassified before the April BvA can be trusted.
recap_notes: GRP can absorb this week's $5,329 in maintenance bills without intercompany transfers.
Country Aire and Winterwood need watching. Hold any non-critical bills next week.
Lien release timing tied to sale closings remains the biggest forward risk.
Confirm with Trent on Thursday whether Tim Feb distribution moves or holds.
April expense variance at Countryside, Riverside, RMF needs receipt cleanup before month-end close.
